course_description:
This course presents students with the concepts and methodologies of occupational hygiene, including the anticipation, recognition, evaluation, communication and control of environmental stressors in, or arising from, the workplace that may result in injury, illness, impairment, or affect the well-being of workers and members of the community. Through a range of lectures, tutorials, practicals and field visits, students will develop a high level understanding of the techniques for measuring and monitoring exposure to hazardous chemicals in the workplace and how exposure information can be used to assess risk. As part of this learning, students will develop skills needed to present the results of atmospheric monitoring in a form useful for health risk assessment purposes. The course will present the principles of control of occupational health hazards, including chemical, biological and physical hazards, and will require students to critically appraise these strategies. There will be a focus on three major types of occupational hygiene hazards: particulates (dusts, fumes and fibres); metals; and gases and vapours. Toxicological principles relevant to developing an understanding of workplace health hazards will also be reviewed such as dose response relationships, types of toxic effects and the elimination and detoxification of chemicals and biological agents.

OHSS7002 has been designed to provide students with an introduction to the concepts and methodologies of occupational hygiene, including the anticipation, recognition, evaluation, communication and control of environmental stressors in, or arising from, the workplace that may result in injury, illness, impairment, or affect the well-being of workers and members of the community. Through a range of lectures, practicals and field visits, students will develop an understanding of the techniques for measuring and monitoring exposure to hazardous chemicals in the workplace and how exposure information can be used to assess risk and determine and implement appropriate methods of control.ﾠAs part of this learning, students will develop skills needed to present and interpret the results of atmospheric monitoring. The course will focus on three major types of occupational hygiene hazards: particulates (dusts, fumes and fibres); metals; and gases and vapours. Basic toxicological principles relevant to developing an understanding of workplace health hazards will also be examined such as dose response relationships, types of toxic effects and the elimination and detoxification of chemicals.

Additional timetable information
This course will be delivered over two, three-day face-to-face blocks. The blocks will consist of lectures, workshops, and practicals.
course_aims: The course aims to introduce students to the broad principles of occupational hygiene, including anticipation, recognition, evaluation and control of workplace health hazards. Focusing on the techniques for measuring, monitoring, assessing and controlling airborne contaminants.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of and an ability to critically apply occupational hygiene principles to the anticipation, recognition, evaluation, communication and control of workplace health hazards.
Review and apply key toxicology principles, exposure standards and basic statistics to the evaluation of complex occupational health and hygiene risks.
Demonstrate an understanding of and an ability to apply the principles of the hierarchy of control to the selection of control measures for a range of complex occupational health hazards.
Select appropriate equipment to measure specific airborne contaminants and devise suitable sampling strategies.
Plan, conduct and prepare a scientific report on a complex survey of a workplace atmospheric contaminant in a manner useful for health risk assessment purposes.
Apply occupational hygiene principles to the completion of hazardous chemical risk assessments.

assessment_details.title: Problem solution
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be presented with a number of typical occupational hygiene issues and asked to prepare a response based on the scenario provided. The problem will require students to further develop and apply their knowledge and understanding of hazardous chemical monitoring and control. The assignment has a maximum word length of 2500 words. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets required.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
See the Additional assessment information section further below for information relating to extension and deferral applications.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date. For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period).

assessment_details.title: Practical Report
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be required to prepare a scientific report based on the completion of a workplace occupational hygiene survey. The report is to be prepared in the style of a hygiene consultant's report with the aim of providing useful information for the purpose of completing a health risk assessment. The assignment has a maximum word length of 3000. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

Each student will be assessed individually. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.

learning_activities.topic: Module 1: Introduction to occupational hygiene and revision of toxicological principles #1 Students are to independently complete the following H5P modules accessible via the Blackboard page before attendance at the first contact session: Unit 1: History and Introduction to Occupational Hygiene Unit 2: Revision of Key Toxicological Principles for Occupational Hygiene Practice Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Module 1: Introduction to occupational hygiene and revision of toxicological principles #2 This course will be delivered over two, three-day face-to-face blocks. The blocks will consist of lectures, workshops, and practicals. During the lectures and workshops for this module, students will gain an understanding of the principles and practice of occupational hygiene and will revise key toxicological principles required for the practice of occupational hygiene. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Module 2: Occupational hygiene process This course will be delivered over two, three-day face-to-face blocks. The blocks will consist of lectures, workshops, and practicals. During the lectures and workshops for this module, students will gain an understanding of the occupational hygiene process and the various strategies employed in the conduct of hygiene monitoring, including personal, workplace, and biological sample collection. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Module 3: Workplace Exposure Limits (WELs) This course will be delivered over two, three-day face-to-face blocks. The blocks will consist of lectures, workshops, and practicals. During the lectures and workshops for this module, students will gain an understanding of the development, review and update of workplace exposure limits (WELs) and importantly the practical application of WELs in occupational hygiene. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L06

Module 4: Statistics This course will be delivered over two, three-day face-to-face blocks. The blocks will consist of lectures, workshops, and practicals. During the lectures and workshops for this module, students will gain an understanding of the statistical techniques used to analyse, interpret, and present the results from occupational hygiene monitoring. Learning outcomes: L01, L05

Module 8: Report writing, communication and ethics This course will be delivered over two, three-day face-to-face blocks. The blocks will consist of lectures, workshops, and practicals. During the lectures and workshops for this module, students will gain an understanding of, and ability to apply, the principles of occupational hygiene to the communication of occupational hygiene with various stakeholders, including workers, management, and government. Students will also develop an ability to write occupational hygiene reports in line with the requirements of the AIOH Report Writing Guideline and to consider the ethical implications of the role of an occupational hygienist. Learning outcomes: L01, L05, L06
